events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to assess a scenario: EMT bending & installation, observed condition: kinked/flattened bend. Work stage: rough-complete. The task: estimate or interpret the measurable condition and state why it is out of tolerance. We need to output JSON with decision, risk, etc.

First, understand the condition: a kinked or flattened bend in EMT (Electrical Metallic Tubing). According to NEC Article 358, specifically 358.24 Bends — How Made. Bends shall be made so that the tubing is not damaged and the internal diameter is not effectively reduced. A kinked or flattened bend would damage the tubing and reduce the internal diameter, potentially causing issues with wire pulling and fill. Also, Chapter 9 Table 1 notes that the internal diameter of the conduit must not be reduced. So this is a defect. The work is roughed in, so it's installed but not yet closed out. The condition is "installed-defective". The decision should be "fail" because it's non-compliant and could be dangerous (damaged conduit may have sharp edges, reduced fill capacity, etc.). Risk: medium? Possibly high if it's a critical circuit, but generally a kinked bend is a defect that needs correction. I'll set risk to "medium" as it's a code violation but not an immediate life safety hazard unless it's in a hazardous location. But the scenario doesn't specify, so medium is reasonable. Work stage: "rough-complete". Percent complete: maybe 90%? Rough-in is done, but not final. I'll estimate 90. Value: no numeric reading, so 0. Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time: 0. Rate: 0. Order: empty. Workable: empty. Findings: "Kinked or flattened EMT bend reduces internal diameter and may damage conductors." Actions: "Replace the damaged section of EMT with a properly bent section." Rationale: "NEC 358.24 requires bends to be made without damaging the tubing or effectively reducing the internal diameter. A kinked/flattened bend violates this." References: ["NEC 358.24", "NEC Chapter 9 Table 1"].

Component conditions: ["installed-defective"] because it's installed and defective. Possibly "non-compliant" as well? The schema says "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant", "worn", "degraded", "failed"]. I'll include both "installed-defective" and "non-compliant" since it's a code violation. But the instruction says "list the condition categories that apply to the component". So I'll put ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"].

Decision: "fail". Risk: "medium". Work_stage: "rough-complete". Percent_complete: 90. Value: 0. Sound_source: "". Confidence: 0.95. Event_time: 0. Rate: 0. Order: []. Workable: []. Findings: ["Kinked or flattened EMT bend reduces internal diameter and may damage conductors."]. Actions: ["Replace the damaged section of EMT with a properly bent section."]. Rationale: "NEC 358.24 requires bends to be made without damaging the tubing or effectively reducing the internal diameter. A kinked/flattened bend violates this." References: ["NEC 358.24", "NEC Chapter 9 Table 1"].

I'll output the JSON.
